bins.name: Holistic joint load-generation study
bins.desc: Google argues that studying large loads and associated generation together, rather than in isolation, is essential to identify efficient network upgrades and reduce interconnection timelines. The current reactive, siloed approach produces piecemeal infrastructure that cannot keep pace with demand.
quotes: Inadequate electric transmission infrastructure is becoming the principal limitation to interconnecting new large loads, including data centers, and the needed generation capacity at the speed and scale necessary to sustain our global economic competitiveness.
it takes too long to interconnect new large loads and new generation, and the cost of doing so is too high. Lengthy interconnection delays are directly driving up energy prices.

the record is clear: to get large load interconnection right, the Commission must consider the interplay between transmission capacity and the interconnection of both large loads and the generators that supply them.

The way to address that foundational transmission issue is to adopt a holistic transmission planning process that optimizes grid development by accounting for both generators and the large loads planning to interconnect.

bins.name: Cut upgrade costs via efficient sizing
bins.desc: Google supports sizing network upgrades and transmission service based on actual injection and withdrawal rights rather than gross load. This approach sends better price signals to developers and reduces unnecessary upgrade costs for ratepayers.
quotes: Inadequate electric transmission infrastructure is becoming the principal limitation to interconnecting new large loads, including data centers, and the needed generation capacity at the speed and scale necessary to sustain our global economic competitiveness.
it takes too long to interconnect new large loads and new generation, and the cost of doing so is too high. Lengthy interconnection delays are directly driving up energy prices.

Determining what network upgrades are required to accommodate the actual injection and withdrawal rights requested and providing transmission service based on the actual use of the transmission system will send the proper signals to encourage developers to pursue the most efficient projects.

bins.name: Expedite co-location study pathway
bins.desc: Google supports an expedited, coordinated study process for loads and generation behind the same point of interconnection. Co-location minimizes dependence on constrained transmission corridors and raises fewer jurisdictional questions than standalone load interconnection.
quotes: The concerns raised in the record about co-location are readily resolvable, and do not outweigh its potential value in interconnecting new large loads in a manner that maximizes the use of existing infrastructure and minimizes strain on the grid.

Determining what network upgrades are required to accommodate the actual injection and withdrawal rights requested and providing transmission service based on the actual use of the transmission system will send the proper signals to encourage developers to pursue the most efficient projects.

bins.name: Incentivize voluntary curtailable load service
bins.desc: Google supports creating an optional expedited interconnection pathway for loads willing to curtail during system stress. Research cited by Google shows that even a 0.25% annual curtailment rate could accommodate 76 GW of new load, and flexible connections in PJM could cut wait times by three to five years.
quotes: a load curtailment rate of just 0.25% annually would allow for the integration of 76 GW of new load—the equivalent of 10% of the nation's current aggregate peak demand—with minimal generation capacity expansion.

combining flexible grid connections with a "bring-your-own-capacity" framework in PJM could shorten interconnection wait times by three to five years and maintain grid power availability for over 99% of hours, while requiring on-site resource dispatch for only 40 to 70 hours annually.

Rules governing the interconnection and operation of curtailable loads must account for how large loads, including data centers, operate in practice. Such rules must encourage innovation without being so onerous and/or impractical that they discourage large loads from opting to operate flexibly when doing so could be beneficial for the load and the system.

bins.name: Expedite electrically proximate generation pairing
bins.desc: Google endorses an optional, time-limited expedited study path for generation electrically proximate to a new large load, modeled on SPP's HILLGA proposal. The pathway must be technology neutral, open to storage, and must not block loads that cannot find a proximate generator.
quotes: Google agrees with commenters that the Commission should establish pathways for facilitating the expedited study and interconnection of electrically proximate pairs of generation and load, as doing so will reduce the network upgrades needed to interconnect new resources, which can accelerate the pace at which new generation comes online.

the Commission should ensure that any such regime: Provides an optional pathway (i.e., an incentive) and not a requirement that effectively blocks the interconnection of large loads that, for whatever reason, are not or cannot be paired with electrically proximate generation.

Ensures that any expedited interconnection process is technology neutral. In particular, it must be open to storage resources, which can deploy more rapidly than any other grid resource and can manage the impact of large loads on the grid.

bins.name: Joint load and generation studies
bins.desc: Google agrees with the DOE ANOPR framing that load and generation interconnection requests that are related should be studied together, aligning project timelines and reducing piecemeal upgrade decisions.
quotes: the record is clear: to get large load interconnection right, the Commission must consider the interplay between transmission capacity and the interconnection of both large loads and the generators that supply them.

The way to address that foundational transmission issue is to adopt a holistic transmission planning process that optimizes grid development by accounting for both generators and the large loads planning to interconnect.

bins.name: FERC has authority, states keep retail role
bins.desc: Google accepts FERC's interconnection jurisdiction but cautions against overstating it. States retain exclusive authority over retail rates and must be actively involved in every large load interconnection. Google's preferred reforms cluster at the less contested end of the jurisdictional spectrum.
quotes: Although Google agrees that the Commission has jurisdiction over the actual interconnection of large loads to the transmission system, it is important not to overstate the practical impact of that conclusion in the broader context of large load integration issues. Because states have exclusive jurisdiction over the rates, terms, and conditions of retail sales, every large load interconnection in the country will require active state involvement.

The substantive reforms discussed above that Google is advocating for in this pleading fall on the relatively uncontroversial end of the spectrum. Accordingly, those reforms not only minimize strain on the existing grid, but they also minimize jurisdictional friction between the Commission and the states, which Google believes will further help ensure their effectiveness, particularly in the near term.

bins.name: Net-load rights for hybrid facilities
bins.desc: Google agrees with CAISO that transmission service for co-located facilities should be based on net injection and withdrawal rights, not gross load. This correctly incentivizes developers to right-size their requests and avoids unnecessary upgrades.
quotes: The concerns raised in the record about co-location are readily resolvable, and do not outweigh its potential value in interconnecting new large loads in a manner that maximizes the use of existing infrastructure and minimizes strain on the grid.

Determining what network upgrades are required to accommodate the actual injection and withdrawal rights requested and providing transmission service based on the actual use of the transmission system will send the proper signals to encourage developers to pursue the most efficient projects.

bins.name: Expedited interconnection pathways
bins.desc: Google urges the Commission to act promptly to create expedited interconnection pathways for co-located, electrically proximate, and curtailable configurations. Study timelines of 90 days and defined negotiation windows are recommended.
quotes: Google agrees with commenters that the Commission should establish pathways for facilitating the expedited study and interconnection of electrically proximate pairs of generation and load, as doing so will reduce the network upgrades needed to interconnect new resources, which can accelerate the pace at which new generation comes online.

combining flexible grid connections with a "bring-your-own-capacity" framework in PJM could shorten interconnection wait times by three to five years and maintain grid power availability for over 99% of hours, while requiring on-site resource dispatch for only 40 to 70 hours annually.

bins.name: PJM flexible connection model
bins.desc: Google cites Princeton ZERO Lab research showing that a flexible grid connection combined with a bring-your-own-capacity framework in PJM could cut interconnection wait times by three to five years with minimal curtailment burden.
quotes: combining flexible grid connections with a "bring-your-own-capacity" framework in PJM could shorten interconnection wait times by three to five years and maintain grid power availability for over 99% of hours, while requiring on-site resource dispatch for only 40 to 70 hours annually.

bins.name: Transmission investment as the core fix
bins.desc: Google frames inadequate and siloed transmission planning as the root cause of interconnection delays and rising energy costs. Long-term, the Commission must support holistic regional transmission planning that accounts for both load and generation growth.
quotes: Inadequate electric transmission infrastructure is becoming the principal limitation to interconnecting new large loads, including data centers, and the needed generation capacity at the speed and scale necessary to sustain our global economic competitiveness.

The way to address that foundational transmission issue is to adopt a holistic transmission planning process that optimizes grid development by accounting for both generators and the large loads planning to interconnect.

bins.name: Technology-neutral expedited paths
bins.desc: Google insists that any fast-track interconnection pathway must treat co-location, flexible load, and storage on equal footing. A pathway that favors one resource type over another would be arbitrary and could distort investment decisions.
quotes: any expedited processes are technology neutral and do not arbitrarily favor loads paired with generation over those capable of providing equivalent relief through flexibility.

the Commission should ensure that any such regime: Provides an optional pathway (i.e., an incentive) and not a requirement that effectively blocks the interconnection of large loads that, for whatever reason, are not or cannot be paired with electrically proximate generation.

Ensures that any expedited interconnection process is technology neutral. In particular, it must be open to storage resources, which can deploy more rapidly than any other grid resource and can manage the impact of large loads on the grid.

bins.name: Workable curtailment rule design
bins.desc: Google argues that curtailment rules must reflect how data centers actually operate. Rules that are too onerous or impractical will deter participation and undercut the grid benefits that flexible load service is meant to deliver.
quotes: Rules governing the interconnection and operation of curtailable loads must account for how large loads, including data centers, operate in practice. Such rules must encourage innovation without being so onerous and/or impractical that they discourage large loads from opting to operate flexibly when doing so could be beneficial for the load and the system.

bins.name: Federal-state jurisdictional balance
bins.desc: Google accepts FERC's authority over interconnection to the transmission system but stresses that states retain exclusive retail regulatory authority. Google argues its preferred reforms sit at the less jurisdictionally contested end of the spectrum, minimizing friction between federal and state regulators.
quotes: Although Google agrees that the Commission has jurisdiction over the actual interconnection of large loads to the transmission system, it is important not to overstate the practical impact of that conclusion in the broader context of large load integration issues. Because states have exclusive jurisdiction over the rates, terms, and conditions of retail sales, every large load interconnection in the country will require active state involvement.

The substantive reforms discussed above that Google is advocating for in this pleading fall on the relatively uncontroversial end of the spectrum. Accordingly, those reforms not only minimize strain on the existing grid, but they also minimize jurisdictional friction between the Commission and the states, which Google believes will further help ensure their effectiveness, particularly in the near term.
